title: The Master's Shortcut
description: Two wrong publications sit on the board. One ingredient is definitively known — use master mixes to expose both lies.

hints.text: A master debunk succeeds when one ingredient's alchemical is definitively known — the audience can then check whether the expected mix result (computed from that known alchemical and the claimed alchemical of a publication) contradicts the true result. ing1 is fully established by the three aspect clues. Look for publications that a master mix involving ing1 can expose.

ing1 is known to be npN. The true mix of ing1 + ing3 is B-. The publication for ing3 claims it is Nnp — if that were true, mix(npN, Nnp) = R-, which contradicts B-. So ing3's publication is exposed by a master mix of ing1 + ing3. Similarly, the publication for ing5 claims it is pNn — mix(npN, pNn) = B-, but the true result of ing1 + ing5 is R-. Contradiction.

Step 1: Master debunk — mix ing1 + ing3. ing1 is definitively known (npN); the claimed alch for ing3 (Nnp) predicts R-, but the true result is B- → ing3's publication removed. Step 2: Master debunk — mix ing1 + ing5. The claimed alch for ing5 (pNn) predicts B-, but the true result is R- → ing5's publication removed.
